---
title: "Visible Explorer"
subtitle: "Upload your Visible export and explore your tracking data — privately"
engine: knitr
format:
  live-html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
webr:
  cell-options:
    autorun: true
    echo: false
    warning: false
---

{{< include _extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

```{webr}
#| label: webr-prewarm
#| output: false

suppressMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(lubridate)
  library(forcats)
  library(ggdendro)
  library(changepoint)
  library(patchwork)
})
invisible(NULL)
```

{{< include _helpers.qmd >}}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Your data never leaves your browser.**
This page runs R locally in your browser using [WebR](https://docs.r-wasm.org/webr/).
The CSV you upload is processed in-memory on your machine — nothing is sent to a server.
:::

## Upload your data

Export your data from the [Visible app](https://www.makevisible.com/) (**Settings → Export Data**), then drop the resulting `visible.csv` here.

::: {.upload-loader}

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-file-picker
//| echo: false
viewof file = Inputs.file({
  label: "Visible CSV export",
  accept: ".csv,.txt"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-sample-button
//| echo: false
viewof sampleClick = {
  const btn = html`<button type="button" class="load-sample" aria-label="Load sample data">
    <span class="load-sample-eyebrow">Or try it first</span>
    <span class="load-sample-label">Load sample data</span>
    <span class="load-sample-hint">three years of synthetic tracking</span>
  </button>`;
  let count = 0;
  btn.value = 0;
  btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    count += 1;
    btn.value = count;
    btn.classList.add('loading');
    btn.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('input', { bubbles: true }));
  });
  return btn;
}
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-sample-text
//| echo: false
//| output: false
sampleText = {
  if (!(Number(sampleClick) > 0)) return "";
  const r = await fetch('sample-visible-multiyear.csv');
  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`Sample CSV failed to load (${r.status})`);
  return await r.text();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-file-text
//| echo: false
//| output: false
fileText = file ? await file.text() : sampleText
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-has-data
//| echo: false
//| output: false
hasData = {
  if (fileText) {
    document.body.classList.add('has-data');
  } else {
    document.body.classList.remove('has-data');
  }
  return fileText ? true : false;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-dark-mode
//| echo: false
//| output: false
darkMode = Generators.observe(notify => {
  const read = () => notify(document.body.classList.contains('quarto-dark'));
  read();
  const obs = new MutationObserver(read);
  obs.observe(document.body, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: ['class'] });
  return () => obs.disconnect();
})
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-data-ready-class
//| echo: false
//| output: false
dataReadyClass = {
  if (dataStatus && dataStatus.stage === 'ready') {
    document.body.classList.add('data-ready');
  } else {
    document.body.classList.remove('data-ready');
  }
  return null;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-data-status
//| echo: false
//| output: false
dataStatus = {
  if (!fileText) return { stage: 'idle' };
  return Generators.observe(notify => {
    notify({ stage: 'loading', message: 'Booting R…' });
    let lastMessage = 'Booting R…';
    const allLines = () => {
      const cells = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="webr-"]');
      const out = [];
      cells.forEach(c => {
        c.textContent.split(/\r?\n/).forEach(l => {
          const t = l.trim();
          if (t.length > 0) out.push(t);
        });
      });
      return out;
    };
    const check = () => {
      const lines = allLines();
      const readyLine = [...lines].reverse().find(l => l.startsWith('READY '));
      if (readyLine) {
        notify({ stage: 'ready', message: readyLine.replace(/^READY\s+/, '') });
        obs.disconnect();
        clearInterval(interval);
        return;
      }
      const stepLine = [...lines].reverse().find(l => l.startsWith('STEP '));
      if (stepLine) {
        const msg = stepLine.replace(/^STEP\s+/, '');
        if (msg !== lastMessage) {
          lastMessage = msg;
          notify({ stage: 'loading', message: msg });
        }
      }
    };
    const obs = new MutationObserver(check);
    obs.observe(document.body, {
      childList: true, subtree: true, characterData: true
    });
    const interval = setInterval(check, 300);
    check();
    return () => { obs.disconnect(); clearInterval(interval); };
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-upload-status
//| echo: false
html`<div class="upload-status ${fileText ? 'active' : 'idle'}">
  ${!fileText
    ? html`<em>No file picked yet — choose your <code>visible.csv</code> above, or load the sample data to take it for a spin.</em>`
    : dataStatus.stage === 'ready'
      ? html`
          <div class="upload-status-row">
            <span class="upload-check" aria-hidden="true">✓</span>
            <strong>Your data is loaded.</strong>
          </div>
          <div class="upload-status-help">
            ${dataStatus.message} Charts are filling in below as each one finishes.
          </div>`
      : html`
          <div class="upload-status-row">
            <span class="spinner"></span>
            <strong>${dataStatus.message}</strong>
          </div>
          <div class="upload-progress">
            <progress aria-label="Loading your data"></progress>
          </div>
          <div class="upload-status-help">
            R is starting up in your browser, then it'll read the data and build each chart in turn. The first load can take a minute or two — longer if you've used Visible for several years. Feel free to leave this tab open and do something else; it'll keep working in the background and the charts will appear as they're ready.
          </div>`}
</div>`
```

::: {.empty-state-hint}
Once you upload, you'll get a tour through your data in six short chapters: how much you've been logging and where, how your stability and heart-rate measures have moved over time, which trackers tend to travel together, when things seemed to shift, how your menstrual cycle and functional capacity look, and tools to dig into a single tracker, compare two, or revisit your toughest days.
None of it leaves your machine.
:::

::: {.analysis-section}

```{webr}
#| label: setup-data
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (!nzchar(fileText)) {
  invisible(NULL)
} else {
  step("Parsing your CSV file…")
  raw <- read.csv(text = fileText, stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                  check.names = FALSE)
  names(raw) <- trimws(names(raw))
  required <- c("observation_date", "tracker_name",
                "tracker_category", "observation_value")
  missing <- setdiff(required, names(raw))
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop("Uploaded file is missing column(s): ",
         paste(missing, collapse = ", "),
         "\nMake sure you exported the file from Settings → Export Data.")
  }

  step("Extracting notes and trackers…")
  notes_df <- raw |>
    filter(tracker_name == "Note",
                  !is.na(observation_value),
                  nchar(observation_value) > 0) |>
    transmute(
      odate = as.Date(observation_date),
      note = observation_value
    ) |>
    arrange(desc(odate))

  visible <- raw |>
    filter(tracker_name != "Note") |>
    transmute(
      odate = as.Date(observation_date),
      category = tracker_category,
      tracker = tracker_name,
      value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(observation_value))
    )

  visible$tracker[visible$tracker == "HRV"] <- "HR Variability"

  step("Working out time spans and recent window…")
  data_span_days <- as.integer(diff(range(visible$odate, na.rm = TRUE)))
  is_multi_year <- data_span_days >= 540
  recent_cutoff <- if (is_multi_year) {
    max(visible$odate, na.rm = TRUE) - 365
  } else {
    min(visible$odate, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  recent_label <- if (is_multi_year) "your last 12 months" else "all your data"
  visible_recent <- visible[visible$odate >= recent_cutoff, ]

  cp_Q <- max(2L, min(6L,
                       as.integer(round(data_span_days / 365 * 2))))

  is_funcap <- grepl("^Funcap", visible$category)
  n_trackers_real <- length(unique(visible$tracker[!is_funcap]))
  n_cats_real <- length(unique(visible$category[!is_funcap]))

  step("Detecting your most stable and hardest stretches…")
  best_stretch <- NULL
  worst_stretch <- NULL
  stab_for_runs <- visible[visible$tracker == "Stability Score" &
                              !is.na(visible$value), ]
  if (nrow(stab_for_runs) >= 21) {
    daily_stab <- aggregate(value ~ odate, stab_for_runs, mean)
    all_d <- data.frame(odate = seq.Date(min(daily_stab$odate),
                                          max(daily_stab$odate), 1))
    full_stab <- merge(all_d, daily_stab, by = "odate", all.x = TRUE)
    full_stab <- full_stab[order(full_stab$odate), ]
    n_full <- nrow(full_stab)
    half <- 3L
    stab_roll <- rep(NA_real_, n_full)
    for (i in seq_len(n_full)) {
      lo <- max(1L, i - half); hi <- min(n_full, i + half)
      vals <- full_stab$value[lo:hi]
      if (sum(!is.na(vals)) >= 4) stab_roll[i] <- mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
    }
    overall_stab_med <- stats::median(stab_roll, na.rm = TRUE)
    margin_stab <- 0.25
    above_flag <- !is.na(stab_roll) & stab_roll > overall_stab_med + margin_stab
    below_flag <- !is.na(stab_roll) & stab_roll < overall_stab_med - margin_stab

    best_stretch  <- longest_run(above_flag, full_stab$odate, stab_roll)
    worst_stretch <- longest_run(below_flag, full_stab$odate, stab_roll)
    stab_overall_median <- overall_stab_med
  }

  done(sprintf(
    "%s observations across %s days · %s trackers · %s categories.",
    format(nrow(visible), big.mark = ","),
    length(unique(visible$odate)),
    n_trackers_real,
    n_cats_real
  ))
}
```

:::

```{webr}
#| label: prep-matrix
#| input: ["fileText"]
#| output: false

if (exists("visible")) {
  visible_wide <- visible |>
    filter(!grepl("^Funcap", category)) |>
    group_by(odate, category, tracker) |>
    summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
    unite("var", category, tracker, sep = " — ") |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = var, values_from = value)

  visible_mat <- as.matrix(visible_wide[, -1])
  rownames(visible_mat) <- as.character(visible_wide$odate)

  keep <- apply(visible_mat, 2, function(x) {
    n_obs <- sum(!is.na(x))
    n_obs > 5 && stats::var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
  })
  visible_mat_clean <- visible_mat[, keep, drop = FALSE]
}
```

::: {.analysis-section}

```{webr}
#| label: summary-hero
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible")) {
  fmt_date <- function(d) {
    d <- as.Date(d)
    sub("^0", "", format(d, "%d %b %Y"))
  }

  fmt_stretch <- function(s, kind) {
    sprintf(
      "Your <strong>%s stretch</strong> ran <strong>%d days</strong> from <strong>%s</strong>, averaging <strong>%.1f / 5</strong>",
      kind, s$length, fmt_date(s$start), s$mean
    )
  }

  parts <- character(0)
  if (!is.null(best_stretch)) parts <- c(parts, fmt_stretch(best_stretch, "most stable"))
  if (!is.null(worst_stretch)) parts <- c(parts, fmt_stretch(worst_stretch, "hardest"))

  if (length(parts) == 0 && exists("stab_overall_median")) {
    htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
      '<div class="hero-summary"><p>Your stability has stayed broadly steady around <strong>%.1f / 5</strong> — no clear best or hardest stretch stands out.</p></div>',
      stab_overall_median
    ))
  } else if (length(parts) > 0) {
    htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
      '<div class="hero-summary"><p>%s. Stretches are runs of days where your 7-day-smoothed stability stayed clearly above (or below) your typical level (~%.1f / 5).</p></div>',
      paste(parts, collapse = ". "),
      stab_overall_median
    ))
  }
}
```

:::

{{< include _chapters/01-first-glance.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/02-through-time.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/03-what-hangs-together.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/04-where-things-shift.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/05-cycles-capacity.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/06-pacing-recovery.qmd >}}

{{< include _chapters/07-look-closer.qmd >}}


## A few honest reminders

Before you act on anything you've seen here, a few things worth holding in mind.

- **Patterns are not causes.**
  If two trackers move together, it doesn't mean one is causing the other.
  They might both be downstream of something else — sleep, hormones, infection, weather, the stress of the week.
  Treat what you find as questions, not answers.
- **Sparse data is wobbly.**
  Trackers you've only logged a handful of times will produce shaky-looking curves.
  The longer you log, the more solid every chart on this page becomes.
- **Your body is the expert.**
  These charts can surface a pattern you hadn't noticed, but they can't tell you how you feel.
  Cross-check anything striking against your own memory of that period.
- **Bring it to someone who can help.**
  If a chart raises a real concern — a clear downward trend, a change-point that lines up with new symptoms — that's a useful conversation to have with a clinician who knows you.

## What's next

This is a living tool — I'll keep adding lenses as the science (and your suggestions) push it forward.
If there's a chart you wish you could see, the [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/drmowinckels/visible/issues) is the place to ask.

```{=html}
<button class="print-report" type="button" aria-label="Print this report">
  <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
    <polyline points="6 9 6 2 18 2 18 9"></polyline>
    <path d="M6 18H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-5a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h16a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v5a2 2 0 0 1-2 2h-2"></path>
    <rect x="6" y="14" width="12" height="8"></rect>
  </svg>
  <span>Print report</span>
</button>
<script>
(function () {
  function bind() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.print-report').forEach(function (btn) {
      if (btn.dataset.printBound) return;
      btn.dataset.printBound = '1';
      btn.addEventListener('click', function () { window.print(); });
    });
  }
  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', bind);
  } else {
    bind();
  }
})();
</script>
```
